manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: fish, bed, spot, pipe, band, press, game, laser, belt.
Your opponent's team words are: horseshoe, bridge, australia, disease, maple, dance, revolution, plot.
Distractor words are: cell, telescope, cycle, theater, organ, wave, cross, night.
The kill words are: .

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: fish, bed, spot, pipe, band, press, game, laser, belt.
Your opponent's team words are: horseshoe, bridge, australia, disease, maple, dance, revolution, plot.
Distractor words are: cell, telescope, cycle, theater, organ, wave, cross, night.
The kill words are: .

CLUE: GYM
TARGETS: belt, press, band

The words press, belt were guessed correctly. The word cycle was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: fish, bed, spot, pipe, band, game, laser.
Remaining words for your opponent are: horseshoe, bridge, australia, disease, maple, dance, revolution, plot.
Remaining distractor words are: cell, telescope, theater, organ, wave, cross, night.
Remaining kill words are: .

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: fish, bed, spot, pipe, band, press, game, laser, belt.
Your opponent's team words are: horseshoe, bridge, australia, disease, maple, dance, revolution, plot.
Distractor words are: cell, telescope, cycle, theater, organ, wave, cross, night.
The kill words are: .

CLUE: GYM
TARGETS: belt, press, band

The words press, belt were guessed correctly. The word cycle was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: fish, bed, spot, pipe, band, game, laser.
Remaining words for your opponent are: horseshoe, bridge, australia, disease, maple, dance, revolution, plot.
Remaining distractor words are: cell, telescope, theater, organ, wave, cross, night.
Remaining kill words are: .

CLUE: BAG
TARGETS: pipe, bed

The words game, fish were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: bed, spot, pipe, band, laser.
Remaining words for your opponent are: horseshoe, australia, disease, maple, dance, revolution, plot.
Remaining distractor words are: cell, telescope, theater, organ, wave, cross, night.
Remaining kill words are: .

Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Task: Provide a single-word clue and a list of target words from my remaining team words.
    *   Format:
        CLUE: <WORD>
        TARGETS: <TARGETS>
    *   Constraints: Clue must be semantically related, cannot be one of the words or contain parts of them. Short output, no extra text.
    *   Context: This is a Codenames-like game.
    *   Current State:
        *   My remaining team words: bed, spot, pipe, band, laser.
        *   Opponent words: horseshoe, australia, disease, maple, dance, revolution, plot.
        *   Distractor words: cell, telescope, theater, organ, wave, cross, night.
        *   Kill words: None listed (empty).
        *   Previous guesses:
            *   Turn 1: Clue GYM -> guessed belt, press (correct), cycle (distractor, turn ended).
            *   Turn 2: Clue BAG -> guessed game, fish (correct). Wait, the prompt says "The words game, fish were guessed correctly." But my clue was BAG targeting pipe, bed. This implies the partner guessed differently than intended or the prompt is simulating a scenario where I need to react to the new state. Actually, looking closely at the prompt history:
                *   Turn 1 Prompt: Team words included fish, bed, spot, pipe, band, press, game, laser, belt. Clue GYM -> Targets belt, press. Result: belt, press correct, cycle (distractor) guessed. Turn ended.
                *   Turn 2 Prompt: Remaining team words: fish, bed, spot, pipe, band, game, laser. (belt, press removed). Clue BAG -> Targets pipe, bed. Result: "The words game, fish were guessed correctly." This is inconsistent with the clue BAG targeting pipe, bed. However, I must accept the current state as given in the latest prompt.
                *   Turn 3 Prompt (Current): Remaining team words: bed, spot, pipe, band, laser. (fish, game removed).
        *   So I need to clue for: bed, spot, pipe, band, laser.

2.  **Evaluate Remaining Words:**
    *   bed
    *   spot
    *   pipe
    *   band
    *   laser

3.  **Identify Associations:**
    *   *bed*: sleep, rest, room, sheet, mattress, waterbed.

The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: fish, bed, spot, pipe, band, press, game, laser, belt.
